title: chionochloa rigida subsp. amara
intro: Chionochloa rigida subsp. amara, also known as the harsh tussock grass or mountain grass, is a type of plant native to New Zealand. It has been used in traditional Maori medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it useful for treating wounds and skin conditions. The health benefits of Chionochloa rigida subsp. amara include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and potentially even exhibit antifungal and antibacterial properties. Chionochloa rigida subsp. amara is typically prepared as a tea or tincture, and can also be used in topical applications such as salves or ointments.
